question.text: For an inter-se transfer between promoters to be exempt from open offer requirements, for how many years must the transferee have been disclosed in the shareholding pattern?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 10, inter-se transfers between promoters or persons acting in concert are exempt from open offer only if the transferee has been disclosed in the shareholding pattern for at least 3 years and there is no change in control. **The Trap:** Candidates often confuse this with other SEBI time-based requirements. The 3-year disclosure period is specific to inter-se promoter transfer exemptions. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) and compliance-oriented exams test SAST exemption conditions in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When structuring intra-promoter transfers, verify the 3-year disclosure history well in advance — a last-minute addition to the promoter group will disqualify the exemption.
